title: What does label-free holotomography establish?
context: The study evaluates holo-tomographic microscopy (HTM), a label-free method that reconstructs cellular refractive-index maps, and compares it with fluorescence-assisted identification of subcellular structures.
legend_excerpt: Panel A diagrams the HTM setup coupled to epifluorescence. Panel B shows 2D and 3D HTM images of flat, unlabeled, unperturbed HeLa cells and motivates a quantitative strategy for interpreting the image content.
prompt: Identify the workflow and describe what the figure directly demonstrates. Explain what control or orthogonal information would be required to assign a particular refractive-index structure to a named organelle. State the strongest supported conclusion, one overinterpretation, and one high-value follow-up.

source.article_title: Image-based analysis of living mammalian cells using label-free 3D refractive index maps reveals new organelle dynamics and dry mass flux
source.citation: Sandoz PA, Tremblay C, van der Goot FG, Frechin M. PLOS Biology. 2019;17:e3000553.
source.attribution: Figure 1 from Sandoz et al. (2019), PLOS Biology, CC BY.

reference.experiment: A coupled holotomographic and epifluorescence microscopy workflow is used to acquire label-free 2D and 3D refractive-index images of living HeLa cells.
observations: HTM produces high-contrast 2D and 3D representations in which cellular boundaries and multiple internal structures are visible without fluorescent labeling.
The figure demonstrates image acquisition and structural visibility, but it does not by itself establish the molecular identity of each internal structure.
controls_and_statistics: Organelle assignment requires an orthogonal marker, such as a validated fluorescent organelle marker imaged in the same cell, rather than visual resemblance alone.
No statistical comparison or quantitative organelle-identification performance is displayed in this figure.
reference.supported_conclusion: HTM can generate information-rich label-free images of living cells that are suitable for subsequent quantitative analysis, while organelle identities require independent validation.
reference.unsupported_claim: The figure proves that every high-refractive-index structure is a mitochondrion or lipid droplet and that HTM is superior to all fluorescence microscopy methods.
reference.follow_up: Co-register HTM with validated fluorescent markers for several organelles across multiple cells, quantify classification sensitivity and specificity, and test robustness under perturbations that alter organelle morphology.

observations: high-contrast 2D and 3D representations
multiple internal structures are visible
does not establish the molecular identity of each internal structure
controls_and_statistics: validated fluorescent organelle markers
no statistical comparison is shown
supported_conclusion: information-rich label-free images suitable for quantitative analysis
unsupported_claim: every high-refractive-index structure is a mitochondrion or lipid droplet
superior to all fluorescence microscopy methods
follow_up: co-register HTM with fluorescent organelle markers

prohibited_claims: every high refractive index structure is a mitochondrion
HTM is superior to all fluorescence microscopy
contradictory_claims: organelle identity is proven by morphology alone

title: Interpreting motor-neuron axon-guidance phenotypes in lron-11 mutants
context: The figure tests whether loss of the extracellular leucine-rich-repeat protein LRON-11 affects ventral nerve cord tract placement, commissural laterality, and dorsal nerve cord continuity in C. elegans motor neurons.
legend_excerpt: Panels A-C use DD/VD motor-neuron markers: wild type shows neurites in the right ventral nerve cord tract, whereas mutants can show neurites in the left tract or a commissure on the incorrect side. Panels D-E compare DD/VD dorsal nerve cord continuity and show a mutant gap. Panels F-G use a DA/DB motor-neuron marker and show mutant commissures extending on the incorrect side. Anterior is left; scale bars are 20 micrometres.
prompt: Identify the reporter and phenotype shown in each panel group (A-C, D-E, and F-G). Compare wild-type and mutant morphology, identify the control, and distinguish direct observations from mechanistic claims. State the strongest supported conclusion, one overinterpretation, and a neuron-class-specific rescue experiment that would test cell autonomy.

source.article_title: lron-11 guides axons in the ventral nerve cord of Caenorhabditis elegans
source.citation: Kokan N, Witt S, Sandhu S, Hutter H. PLOS ONE. 2022;17:e0278258.
source.attribution: Figure 4 from Kokan et al. (2022), PLOS ONE, CC BY.

reference.experiment: Fluorescent DD/VD and DA/DB motor-neuron reporters are used to compare ventral nerve cord tract placement, commissural laterality, and dorsal nerve cord continuity in wild-type and lron-11 mutant C. elegans.
observations: Panels A-C show wild-type DD/VD neurites in the right ventral tract, while mutant neurites can enter the left tract and a DD/VD commissure can extend on the incorrect side.
Panels D-E show continuous wild-type DD/VD dorsal nerve cord signal and a gap in the mutant dorsal nerve cord.
Panels F-G use a DA/DB reporter and show mutant DA/DB commissures extending on the incorrect side.
controls_and_statistics: Wild-type animals imaged with the corresponding neuronal reporter provide the primary morphological controls.
Representative images establish phenotype classes but do not by themselves establish penetrance or statistical significance.
reference.supported_conclusion: Loss of lron-11 is associated with defects in motor-neuron ventral tract placement, commissural laterality, and dorsal nerve cord continuity.
reference.unsupported_claim: The images alone prove that LRON-11 directly senses a specific extracellular guidance cue or acts cell autonomously in DD/VD or DA/DB neurons.
reference.follow_up: Perform DD/VD- and DA/DB-specific rescue of lron-11 in the null background with expression-matched controls, then quantify tract placement, commissural laterality, and dorsal nerve cord continuity.

experiment: wild-type and lron-11 mutant C. elegans
DD VD and DA DB motor-neuron reporters
observations: wild-type DD VD neurites in the right ventral tract
mutant DD VD neurites in the left ventral tract
DD VD commissure extends on the incorrect side
dorsal nerve cord gap in the mutant
DA DB commissures extend on the incorrect side
controls_and_statistics: wild type with the same neuronal reporter is the morphological control
representative images do not establish penetrance or statistical significance
supported_conclusion: loss of lron-11 is associated with motor-neuron axon-navigation defects
ventral tract placement commissural laterality or dorsal nerve cord continuity is disrupted
unsupported_claim: directly senses a specific extracellular guidance cue
acts cell autonomously in DD VD or DA DB neurons
follow_up: DD VD and DA DB specific rescue of lron-11

prohibited_claims: LRON 11 directly senses a specific extracellular guidance cue
contradictory_claims: panel C shows branching or looping
panel E shows varicosities or branch points
panels F and G are DD VD neurons

title: Benchmarking a single-cell nascent-RNA sequencing workflow
context: The figure introduces single-cell global run-on sequencing (scGRO-seq), which captures nascent transcription in isolated nuclei, and benchmarks aggregate and single-cell measurements against orthogonal assays.
legend_excerpt: The panels show the scGRO-seq workflow, representative gene and enhancer tracks, reads per cell, correlations with aggregate inAGTuC and intron seqFISH measurements, and the distribution of reads across genomic regions. Reported correlations include r-squared values of approximately 0.93 and 0.99 for aggregate gene-body and enhancer comparisons, and 0.58 for the single-cell seqFISH comparison.
prompt: Explain what is being benchmarked and what the correlation panels support. Distinguish agreement from identity or superiority, note an important limitation of interpreting correlations from this figure, and propose a follow-up that tests reproducibility or sensitivity at the single-cell level.

source.article_title: Single-cell nascent RNA sequencing unveils coordinated global transcription
source.citation: Mahat DB, Tippens ND, Martin-Rufino JD, et al. Nature. 2024;631:216-223.
source.attribution: Figure 1 from Mahat et al. (2024), Nature, CC BY 4.0.

reference.experiment: scGRO-seq profiles nascent transcription in individually barcoded nuclei and is benchmarked against aggregate nascent-RNA assays and intron seqFISH measurements.
observations: Aggregate scGRO-seq measurements correlate strongly with inAGTuC for gene bodies and enhancers, with displayed r-squared values near 0.93 and 0.99.
Single-cell scGRO-seq and intron seqFISH show a positive but weaker relationship, with a displayed r-squared value near 0.58.
The genomic-region composition differs between scGRO-seq and conventional scRNA-seq, consistent with the methods measuring different RNA populations.
controls_and_statistics: Orthogonal assays provide external comparisons, and the figure reports fitted relationships and r-squared values.
Correlation does not establish unbiased measurement, equivalent sensitivity, or causal biological validity; dynamic range, shared technical biases, and cell-matching assumptions remain relevant.
reference.supported_conclusion: The figure supports that scGRO-seq captures nascent transcription with strong aggregate agreement to orthogonal run-on data and moderate single-cell agreement to intron imaging measurements.
reference.unsupported_claim: The correlations prove that scGRO-seq is error-free, quantitatively identical to every comparator, and superior to scRNA-seq for all biological questions.
reference.follow_up: Run technical and biological replicates with matched nuclei or spike-ins, estimate detection sensitivity and false-negative rates across expression levels, and test whether cell-level measurements reproduce across batches.

experiment: scGRO-seq profiles nascent transcription in individually barcoded nuclei
benchmarked against aggregate nascent-RNA assays and intron seqFISH
observations: strong aggregate correlation for gene bodies and enhancers
weaker single-cell relationship with intron seqFISH
genomic-region composition differs between scGRO-seq and conventional scRNA-seq
controls_and_statistics: orthogonal assays provide external comparisons
correlation does not establish unbiased measurement or equivalent sensitivity
unsupported_claim: superiority to scRNA-seq for all biological questions

prohibited_claims: superior to scRNA seq for all biological questions

title: What does an in vivo single-cell CRISPR screen validate?
context: The study combines in utero delivery of a pooled CRISPR library with mCherry enrichment, single-cell RNA sequencing, and guide capture to follow perturbed epidermal clones at postnatal time points.
legend_excerpt: The figure shows a library targeting 150 recurrently altered cancer genes plus control guides, in utero delivery to Cas9-positive embryos, P4 and P60 sampling, mCherry-positive clone imaging, cell-type annotation by UMAP and markers, and guide detection across major cell populations.
prompt: Describe the experimental design and the validation evidence in the figure. Explain what the control guides and cell-type marker panels contribute. State what cannot yet be concluded about individual genes or tumor formation from this figure alone, and propose the strongest next validation step.

source.article_title: In vivo single-cell CRISPR uncovers distinct TNF programmes in tumour evolution
source.citation: Renz PF, Ghoshdastider U, Baghai Sain S, et al. Nature. 2024;632:419-428.
source.attribution: Figure 1 from Renz et al. (2024), Nature, CC BY 4.0.

reference.experiment: A pooled in vivo CROP-seq screen introduces cancer-gene and non-targeting control sgRNAs into embryonic mouse epidermis, enriches mCherry-positive cells, and jointly measures cell transcriptomes and guide identity at P4 and P60.
observations: mCherry-positive epidermal clones are visible at both postnatal time points.
Single-cell transcriptomes resolve major epidermal and immune populations with expected marker-expression patterns.
Detected guides are distributed across the major annotated cell populations rather than being confined to one cluster.
controls_and_statistics: Non-targeting sgRNAs provide a baseline for library propagation and perturbation-independent behavior.
Cell-type marker genes support cluster annotation, while guide detection across clusters supports broad sampling of perturbed cells.
The figure does not show equivalent editing efficiency for every guide, gene-specific phenotypes, or tumor-forming capacity.
reference.supported_conclusion: The figure validates the overall feasibility of coupling pooled in vivo CRISPR perturbation, epidermal clone recovery, guide capture, and single-cell cell-type annotation.
reference.unsupported_claim: Figure 1 proves that every targeted gene is edited equally and that each enriched clone will progress to cancer through a TNF mechanism.
reference.follow_up: Validate representative sgRNAs individually for editing efficiency and on-target transcriptional effects, reproduce the phenotype with independent guides, and compare each perturbation with non-targeting controls across biological replicates.

observations: mCherry-positive epidermal clones at postnatal time points
major epidermal and immune cell populations
guides detected across major annotated populations
controls_and_statistics: non-targeting sgRNAs provide a baseline
marker genes support cluster annotation
editing efficiency is not established for every guide
supported_conclusion: feasibility of pooled in vivo CRISPR perturbation and single-cell guide capture
unsupported_claim: every targeted gene is edited equally
each enriched clone will progress to cancer through TNF

prohibited_claims: every targeted gene is edited equally
each enriched clone will progress to cancer through TNF
contradictory_claims: guide detection proves equal editing efficiency

title: Calibrating efficacy and safety claims from a mouse dose-response figure
context: The figure evaluates a gemcitabine-paclitaxel drug-combination nanoparticle (GT DcNP) in a 4T1-luc syngeneic mouse model of breast-cancer lung metastasis and compares efficacy-related readouts with body-weight loss.
legend_excerpt: Representative bioluminescence and lung-nodule images are shown across doses. Dose-response curves quantify metastasis inhibition using integrated bioluminescence and nodule counts, while body-weight reduction is used as a gross toxicity measure. Group sizes are 8-15 animals; ED50 and TD50 estimates are fitted in GraphPad Prism, producing an estimated therapeutic index of 15.8.
prompt: Interpret the efficacy and toxicity trends and explain how the therapeutic index was estimated. Identify the control and the limitations of body-weight loss as a safety endpoint. State the strongest conclusion supported for this model, one clinical overclaim, and a follow-up needed before translational claims.

source.article_title: Novel drug combination nanoparticles exhibit enhanced plasma exposure and dose-responsive effects on eliminating breast cancer lung metastasis
source.citation: Mu Q, Yu J, Griffin JI, et al. PLOS ONE. 2020;15:e0228557.
source.attribution: Figure 2 from Mu et al. (2020), PLOS ONE, CC BY.

reference.experiment: Mice bearing 4T1-luc metastatic breast-cancer cells receive increasing doses of GT DcNP, and lung tumor burden and body-weight loss are modeled as efficacy and gross-toxicity dose responses.
observations: Increasing GT DcNP dose is associated with lower bioluminescent tumor burden and fewer visible lung nodules.
Substantial body-weight loss appears at higher doses than the doses associated with metastasis inhibition.
The fitted midpoint ratio of TD50 to averaged ED50 is reported as an estimated therapeutic index of approximately 15.8.
controls_and_statistics: The saline-treated group provides the untreated disease control, and dose groups include 8-15 animals with mean plus or minus SEM displayed.
Body-weight loss is a nonspecific gross toxicity proxy and does not replace clinical chemistry, histopathology, organ-specific toxicity, or repeat-dose safety assessment.
Curve-fit estimates depend on this model, endpoints, formulation, dosing schedule, and the available dose range.
reference.supported_conclusion: In this 4T1-luc mouse model and dosing experiment, GT DcNP shows dose-responsive antimetastatic activity with body-weight loss emerging at higher doses, suggesting a preclinical efficacy-to-gross-toxicity separation.
reference.unsupported_claim: The figure proves that GT DcNP is safe and effective in patients and has a clinical therapeutic index of 15.8.
reference.follow_up: Conduct repeat-dose pharmacokinetic and toxicology studies with clinical chemistry, hematology, histopathology, organ exposure, and efficacy replication in an additional model before inferring a translational safety margin.

experiment: tumor burden and body-weight loss dose responses
observations: increasing dose lowers bioluminescent tumor burden and lung nodules
body-weight loss occurs at higher doses
TD50 to ED50 ratio gives a therapeutic index near 15.8
controls_and_statistics: saline-treated untreated disease control
8 to 15 animals with mean plus or minus SEM
body-weight loss is a nonspecific gross toxicity proxy
supported_conclusion: preclinical efficacy-to-gross-toxicity separation in this mouse model
follow_up: clinical chemistry histopathology and organ exposure

contradictory_claims: body weight proves absence of organ toxicity
therapeutic index is established in humans
the mouse study proves clinical efficacy
